title: Countries and Nationalities
description: Brazil → Brazilian. The US → American. Japan → Japanese. Country ↔ nationality pairs you need every day.
summary_pt_br: Países e nacionalidades em inglês. Em inglês ambos SEMPRE começam com MAIÚSCULA (Brazil, Brazilian). Frase típica: 'I'M FROM Brazil' ou 'I'M Brazilian'.

scenes.text_en:
Mr. Kumar pointed at a world map.
'I was born in India. I'm Indian-American.
Bia is BRAZILIAN. Zara is BRITISH. Joca is BRAZILIAN too.
My wife is JAPANESE. My nephew is MEXICAN. The school? INTERNATIONAL.'
scenes.text_pt:
Mr. Kumar apontou pra um mapa-múndi.
'Nasci na Índia. Sou indo-americano.
Bia é BRASILEIRA. Zara é BRITÂNICA. Joca é BRASILEIRO.
Minha esposa é JAPONESA. Meu sobrinho é MEXICANO. A escola? INTERNACIONAL.'

scenes.title: Two ways to answer 'Where are you from?'
scenes.body_en: 'I'm FROM Brazil.' (country with from). 'I'm BRAZILIAN.' (nationality, no 'from'). Both correct. Never 'I'm from Brazilian' (mixing country with nationality). Capital letters ALWAYS — it's a rule.
scenes.body_pt: Duas formas de responder 'De onde você é?': 'I'm FROM Brazil' ou 'I'm BRAZILIAN'. Nunca misture: 'I'm from Brazilian' está errado. Sempre MAIÚSCULAS.

examples.en: She is from Japan. / She is Japanese.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Welcome to the international festival. Where are you from?
turns.pt: Bem-vindo ao festival internacional. De onde você é?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I am from Brazil. I am Brazilian.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Wonderful. What languages do you speak?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I speak Portuguese and a little English.
turns.pt_hint: 'Falo português e um pouco de inglês.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Have you met our Japanese guests?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: No, I have not. Where are they from in Japan?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: They are from Tokyo. And our French guests are from Paris.
turns.pt: São de Tóquio. Nossos convidados franceses são de Paris.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: That is amazing. Is that Italian food over there?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Yes, and it is delicious. Please try some.
turns.pt: Sim, e está deliciosa. Prove um pouco.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Thank you. I love Italian food.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Countries and Nationalities". Brazil → Brazilian. The US → American. Japan → Japanese. Country ↔ nationality pairs you need every day. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Countries and Nationalities". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Countries and Nationalities'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Countries and Nationalities".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: VOA Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Notícias em inglês lento — ótimo para ouvido iniciante.
